intro: Gothofreda martii, also known as Peruvian mint or Cat's claw mint, is a perennial plant species in the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its minty fragrance and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve stress. The bioactive constituents of Gothofreda martii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
